name: ageism_and_negative_stereotypes
description: Captures the degree to which respondents hold negative stereotypes or discriminatory attitudes toward older adults, including beliefs about their declining health, disengagement, and justification for age-based exclusion from employment.

name: perceived_elder_abuse_and_mistreatment
description: Measures respondents' awareness and beliefs about the nature, causes, and prevalence of abuse and mistreatment directed at older adults, including types of abuse recognized and personal knowledge of family-level abuse.

name: state_and_family_responsibility_for_elder_care
description: Taps into normative beliefs about who bears primary responsibility for the care of older adults — the state, family, or other actors — and the extent to which respondents endorse collective versus familial obligations.

name: assessment_of_older_adult_wellbeing_and_social_conditions
description: Reflects respondents' evaluations of the current and future quality of life, dignity, and social situation of older adults in Mexico, including perceived trends over time and the societal salience of their problems.

name: support_for_elder_friendly_public_policy
description: Measures the extent to which respondents endorse public investment and policy action on behalf of older adults, including willingness to allocate taxes for accessible infrastructure and the perceived importance of specific public policies.
